customer_anxiety_profile: High emotional load — customer is a caregiver for a mother in hospice, is disabled herself, and is managing multiple family stressors simultaneously. She is motivated and cooperative but operating under significant time and logistical pressure. She needs to feel that the closer understands her situation, not just her calendar slot.
unresolved_concerns: Daughter identified as a co-decision-maker and logistics coordinator — not confirmed to be on the appointment
Husband mentioned as someone customer must consult — title co-owner status unknown, not confirmed to be on the appointment
No verify items captured — homeowner status, bill amount, utility company, roof age, prior designs all unknown
Bill collection not completed — lead type unknown; if paid lead, bill is required before sit is valid
Customer's availability is subject to her mother's hospice schedule and her daughter's doctor appointments — appointment time may shift
sensitive_topics_raised: Mother entering hospice care for final weeks of life
Customer's personal disability — does not drive, dependent on husband and daughter for transportation
Customer was ill and missed the prior appointment

exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment rescheduled for Tuesday April 21 at 2:30 PM as a phone call

closer_should_know: This customer's motivation is deeply personal — she wants to reduce her mother's electric bill so her mother can spend her final days at home without financial worry. Lead with acknowledgment of that context before any pitch. The daughter is a likely co-decision-maker who was not included in the appointment; if she is not on the call, the closer should expect a 'I need to talk to my daughter' exit. No qualification data was captured by the setter — treat this as a first-contact qualify-and-close call.
